summary: Large ty/toy.c matching PR that typed and expanded trophy/toy data structures, added many function bodies or cleanup edits, renamed the light-object loader at 0x80306EEC to Toy_LoadLObjList, updated related call sites, and reported progress to 52/70 matched functions. Bot progress reported +0.23% matched code, 13 new matches, 18 unmatched-item improvements, and one .sdata regression.

key_files.role: Primary matching source file; large rewrite adding typed structs, includes, matched and near-matched toy/trophy functions, local control-flow cleanup, stack/codegen tactics, and Toy_LoadLObjList implementation/call-site work.

what_changed: Renamed the global function symbol un_80306EEC to Toy_LoadLObjList in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
PR body states the light-object loader was typed/renamed as Toy_LoadLObjList and call sites were updated.

Expanded toy.c local type knowledge, including ToyListEntry, TyDisplayData list pointers/visible_count, TyLightData archive field, TyLightSymbolEntry, TyLightIndexEntry, TyLightFile, TyCleanupObj, Toy26B8 trophy fields, Toy6E68 floats, ToySubStructS_, TyUnk25, and Ty25Entry.
Added source includes in toy.c for db/db.h, lb/lb_00CE.h, lb/lb_00F9.h, mn/mnmain.h, and dolphin/gx.h.
Replaced some raw pointer arithmetic with typed fields, for example Toy26B8 x19C access in un_80305918.
Simplified un_803067BC from generated-looking unrolled copy logic into forward and reverse loop forms over s16 entries with stride 3.
Added or improved many toy functions; bot report lists 13 new 100% matches and 18 improvements in unmatched items.
Validation evidence in PR body names ninja progress and diff_changes.py; bot progress comment reports matched-code improvement and detailed affected functions.
Bot report also notes one unmatched .sdata regression, from 68.38% to 60.00%.
smart_moves: Kept the symbol rename and call-site updates together so the newly typed loader name Toy_LoadLObjList had corresponding references.
Recovered local structs before deeper function cleanup, making later field access more reviewable than raw offsets.
Used a normal loop in un_803067BC to replace a decompiler-style unrolled copy block, aligning with the natural-loops standard.
Introduced a local default_flags pointer reused across repeated trophy-table branches, reducing repeated address expressions without changing the visible control intent.
Included progress tooling output in PR evidence rather than relying only on source comments.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large matching work in a partially typed file, recovering local structs can unlock multiple nearby improvements and make field accesses easier to reason about.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ToyListEntry, TyDisplayData, TyLightData, TyLightFile, Toy26B8, Toy6E68 struct expansions
text_corpus: bot report lists broad toy.c matching improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: A generated-looking unrolled copy can sometimes be expressed as a simple loop over a strided source pointer while improving match quality.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803067BC replaced 8-at-a-time unrolled reverse copy with for loop and while loop forms
text_corpus: bot report says un_803067BC improved from 82.64% to 95.27%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Function renames are stronger when symbol metadata, header/source typing, and call-site updates are submitted in the same PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt rename to Toy_LoadLObjList
human_pr_text: typing/renaming the light-object loader as Toy_LoadLObjList with updated call sites
changed_files.jsonl: toy.h, tydisplay.c, tyfigupon.c, tylist.c changed

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural loop cleanup improved un_803067BC
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2487, un_803067BC in src/melee/ty/toy.c replaced generated-looking unrolled s16 copy code with ordinary loop forms over a stride-3 source pointer. The bot report showed the function improved from 82.64% to 95.27%, making this a useful historical example of trying natural loops before preserving decompiler-shaped unrolling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803067BC loop rewrite
text_corpus: bot report un_803067BC +37 bytes, 82.64% to 95.27%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed fields can improve toy/trophy matching readability
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2487 expanded local toy/trophy structs and used recovered fields, for example Toy26B8::x19C in un_80305918, instead of raw byte-offset access. The bot report shows un_80305918 reached 100%, supporting this as a local example of typed-field recovery aiding matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Toy26B8 struct expansion
raw_diff: un_80305918 uses ((Toy26B8*) base)->x19C
text_corpus: bot report un_80305918 98.72% to 100.00%

source_update_candidates.title: Document stack-shaping tactics require per-function evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2487 used PAD_STACK and explicit unused stack locals in functions that improved or matched, but the loaded slice only includes broad verification commands and bot progress. This reinforces the existing standard that stack tactics should stay narrow and be backed by specific objdiff/checkdiff evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all 52/70 matched functions are visible in the loaded diff; only the PR body states the aggregate and the bot report lists specific new matches/improvements.
Do not treat the .sdata regression as accepted or harmless; loaded evidence only reports it.
Do not generalize Toy_LoadLObjList naming beyond the specific 0x80306EEC symbol without current source confirmation.
Do not mark remaining M2C_FIELD uses as preferred style; the diff shows them still present, but standards classify them as bridge code when type recovery is incomplete.
